preamble: The elision of [d] is a common stage of lenition in Romance languages. It typically involves an intermediate stage where the stop weakens to a voiced fricative [ð] before disappearing entirely, particularly in intervocalic or word-final positions.

examples.note: Common elision of the past participle suffix in Caribbean and Andalusian Spanish.
Total loss of the intervocalic dental stop.

sources: Williams, E. B. (1938). From Latin to Portuguese: Historical Phonology and Morphology of the Portuguese Language. University of Pennsylvania Press.
